sourceMovie.title: Bolt
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Bolt for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chris Williams keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ice Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bolt clicked for you, Ice Age (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bolt?
faq.answer: Try Ice Age, Puss in Boots: The Last Wish, The Secret Life of Pets 2—each shares DNA with Bolt while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bolt?
faq.answer: Bolt (2008) is best described as Animation, Family, Adventure, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bolt worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
